---
title: "Containers and HPC deployment"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Containers and HPC deployment}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(collapse = TRUE, comment = "#>")
```

popgenVCF supports Docker-compatible OCI images and native Apptainer SIF images. Both deployment paths use the same package code and pinned software environment.

## Docker or Podman

Pull a versioned release:

```bash
docker pull ghcr.io/duceppemo/popgenvcf:0.8.3
```

Generate a configuration and run an analysis from the current directory:

```bash
docker run --rm -v "$PWD:/data" \
  ghcr.io/duceppemo/popgenvcf:0.8.3 \
  --write-config /data/analysis.yml

docker run --rm \
  --user "$(id -u):$(id -g)" \
  -e HOME=/tmp \
  -v "$PWD:/data" \
  ghcr.io/duceppemo/popgenvcf:0.8.3 \
  --config /data/analysis.yml
```

## Apptainer on HPC systems

Convert the published OCI image into a SIF file:

```bash
apptainer pull popgenvcf.sif \
  docker://ghcr.io/duceppemo/popgenvcf:0.8.3
```

Or build from the repository definition:

```bash
apptainer build popgenvcf.sif Apptainer.def
```

Run with an explicit bind mount:

```bash
apptainer run \
  --bind "$PWD:/data" \
  popgenvcf.sif \
  --config /data/analysis.yml
```

## Scheduler example

A minimal Slurm job can derive CPU allocation from the scheduler:

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=8
#SBATCH --mem=32G
#SBATCH --time=08:00:00

apptainer run \
  --bind "$PWD:/data" \
  popgenvcf.sif \
  --config /data/analysis.yml
```

Set the thread count in `analysis.yml` consistently with the scheduler allocation. Avoid requesting more threads than the job receives.

## Verification

The CI pipeline builds and tests the native SIF image. Published OCI images are also pulled back by immutable digest and rerun through package, CLI, and scientific-validation checks.
